title: Binding in hippocampal-entorhinal circuits enables compositionality in cognitive maps
title_zh: 海马体-内嗅皮层环路绑定实现认知地图的组合性
abstract: We propose a normative model for spatial representation in the hippocampal formation that combines optimality principles, such as maximizing coding range and spatial information per neuron, with an algebraic framework for computing in distributed representation. Spatial position is encoded in a residue number system, with individual residues represented by high-dimensional, complex-valued vectors. These are composed into a single vector representing position by a similarity-preserving, conjunctive vector-binding operation. Self-consistency between the vectors representing position and the individual residues is enforced by a modular attractor network whose modules correspond to the grid cell modules in entorhinal cortex. The vector binding operation can also be used to bind different contexts to spatial representations, yielding a model for entorhinal cortex and hippocampus. We provide model analysis of scaling, similarity preservation and convergence behavior as well as experiments demonstrating noise robustness, sub-integer resolution in representing position, and path integration. The model formalizes the computations in the cognitive map and makes testable experimental predictions.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了海马体形成的空间表示规范模型，该模型结合了优化原则（如最大化每个神经元的编码范围和信息量）和用于分布式表示计算的代数框架。空间位置使用余数系统编码，各个余数由高维复值向量表示。这些余数通过相似性保留的合取向量绑定操作组合成表示位置的单一向量。表示位置的向量与各个余数之间的自一致性由一个模块化吸引子网络强制执行，该网络的模块对应于内嗅皮层中的网格细胞模块。向量绑定操作也可用于将不同上下文绑定到空间表示中，从而产生内嗅皮层和海马体的模型。我们提供了模型在缩放、相似性保留和收敛行为方面的分析，以及展示噪声鲁棒性、表示位置的亚整数分辨率和路径整合的实验结果。该模型形式化了认知地图中的计算，并做出了可检验的实验预测。
